strategy: Solo-friendly (16,100 health, no minions). Know the damage cap: it is 500 until he throws his natural attack, then 1,000, 3,000, and finally 9,999 after his third, so early nukes are wasted and this is a setup-heavy grind.
Keep charms off yourself. His round-3 natural attack deals 5% max health plus 5-15% more per charm you carry.
Do not Feint or trap him raw: harmful charms and wards are pushed back to you. Protect a Feint with Indemnity or an Unbalance so it sticks instead.
Attack with Storm (+30%); he resists Myth 47% and everything else 17%. Stun him when available to skip a natural-attack turn.
Build blades on your hitter and unload once the cap has climbed to 9,999.
cheatNotes: He interrupt-casts Equalize to cap all damage, starting at 500 and rising each time he casts his natural attack.
The Equalize is recast if replaced, and the cap does not increase for it.
At end of every third round (sooner with more wizards) he hits a random wizard for 5% max health, plus more per charm you carry.
After that natural attack he gives the target a Rock Put card.
Any harmful charm or ward placed on him is pushed back to the caster.
Infections do not trigger this push-back.
Dispels do not trigger this push-back.
Harmful auras that cut outgoing damage, like Starfall, do trigger it.
Secondary effects from spells trigger it too, but get pushed to a random wizard.
Indemnity and Unbalance can be used to prevent this cheat, so a protected Feint sticks.
